stories.headline: Russia and Ukraine agree to 32-hour Orthodox Easter ceasefire
stories.summary: Moscow and Kyiv signal a short Easter truce as diplomacy stalls and war pressures mount.

stories.headline: Iran war live: Israeli attacks on Lebanon threaten US-Iran ceasefire talks
stories.summary: Iran’s parliament speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf warns ‘time is running out’ amid Israel’s attacks on Lebanon.

stories.headline: Stocks rise and oil prices gain as investors await ceasefire talks
stories.summary: Markets have remained positive, with oil prices edging slightly upward, coupled with gains for global equities ahead of the US-Iran talks in Islamabad on Saturday.

stories.headline: Trump warns Iran over charging for passage through strait
stories.summary: Global stocks rise in wake of S&P winning streak on ceasefire optimism

stories.headline: Trump Attacks Tucker Carlson, Megyn Kelly Over Iran War Criticism
stories.summary: In a lengthy social media post, the president attacked Tucker Carlson, Megyn Kelly and others in starkly personal terms. He also criticized the editorial board of The Wall Street Journal.

stories.headline: Lebanon reeling as Israeli strikes continue after 300 killed yesterday
stories.summary: Israeli air strikes hit Hezbollah sites in southern Lebanon as rocket sirens blare in northern Israel, despite calls to extend a regional ceasefire.

stories.headline: Former Israeli PM: US-Israel-Iran war ceasefire – what next?
stories.summary: In an opinion piece for Euronews, former Israeli Prime Minister Ehud Olmert says the central objectives of the war were not met: the regime did not collapse, enriched uranium remains in Iran’s hands, and it still dominates the Strait of Hormuz. But despite this, there is still a chance for peace.

stories.headline: Petrol and diesel in Europe: Where are prices highest and lowest?
stories.summary: Petrol and diesel prices have risen significantly across Europe since the escalation of the Middle East crisis. Euronews Business takes a closer look at the latest fuel prices.

stories.headline: Taiwan opposition leader meets Xi Jinping in Beijing
stories.summary: Cheng Li-wun is the Kuomintang's first sitting leader to visit China in a decade.

stories.headline: What to Know About the U.S. Military Draft Pool and Automatic Registration
stories.summary: For decades, draft-eligible men ages 18 to 25 have been required to register with the Selective Service System. Most states offer a registration option on driver’s license applications.

stories.summary: Text on plaque in Hall of Valour updated to include references to war crime – murder charges and the ongoing legal processGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastThe Australian War Memorial has updated the display dedicated to Ben Roberts-Smith after the former Special Air Service (SAS) corporal was officially charged with five counts of the war crime of murder.The changes, implemented on Friday, mean nearly half of the descrip

stories.headline: China’s Xi tells Taiwan opposition leader unification an ‘inevitability’
stories.summary: Meeting in Beijing with Chinese president first of its kind in a decade

stories.headline: India Gains More From Peace Than Pakistan
stories.summary: Asia Pacific Foundation Non Resident Senior Fellow Michael Kugelman thinks India will get more benefits than Pakistan from the restoration of peace in the Iran war. He also doesn't agree with the idea that India has been sidelined, or even that, its silence is a bad thing. Kugelman speaks with Menaka Doshi and Haslinda Amin on "Insight with Haslinda Amin." (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: China Reminds Battery Makers to Avoid Excess Capacity Growth
stories.summary: China summoned the country’s leading battery makers for a second time in a little over three months, reinforcing a call to restrict capacity expansion and avoid the price wars that have damaged other renewable-energy industries in the past.

stories.headline: Singapore Minister Warns On Fallout From Iran War
stories.summary: Singapore's Foreign Affairs Minister Vivian Balakrishnan warned the economic fallout from the war in Iran could worsen and markets are yet to factor in the worst-case scenario. In a fireside chat with Bloomberg's Avril Hong at the IMAS Investment Conference in Singapore, he adds that the effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz has disrupted oil and gas shipments to Asia, pushing prices higher and forcing buyers to scramble for alternative suppl

stories.headline: TSMC’s Sales Beat Estimates After War Fails to Dent AI Demand
stories.summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. reported a 35% increase in quarterly revenue, suggesting global AI chip demand remained intact during the first weeks of war in the Middle East.

stories.headline: Riot police push back protesters demanding higher wages in Venezuela
stories.summary: Police pushed back protesters in Venezuela’s capital as they demanded an increase to the minimum wage.

stories.headline: Now would be a great time to send US ambassadors to the Middle East
stories.summary: At a time when many question American leadership, there’s an underrated value in simply showing up

stories.headline: Bessent called in US bank CEOs to discuss Anthropic model’s cyber risks
stories.summary: Meeting with US Treasury secretary comes as latest AI system has detected decades-old vulnerabilities

stories.headline: Indian Bonds Fall as RBI to Drain Cash in First Move This Year
stories.summary: India’s sovereign bond yields rose after the central bank announced its first step this year to drain cash from the banking system, as it seeks to push up overnight borrowing costs to its policy rate.

stories.headline: Indonesia Deploys Thousands of African Bugs to Boost Palm Output
stories.summary: Thousands of tiny bugs from Africa have been released at an Indonesian palm oil plantation in North Sumatra as the world’s biggest grower seeks to revive output growth after years of stalling production.

stories.headline: Horizons Middle East & Africa 4/10/2026
stories.summary: Horizons Middle East & Africa is your daily spotlight on one of the world's fastest-growing regions. Live from Dubai, we bring you the latest global markets and analysis, plus news-making interviews, with a special focus on MEA. All that and more, as you head to the office in the Gulf, pause for lunch in Hong Kong, or start your day in London or Johannesburg.  (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Turkey Overhauls Leadership at State Lenders Halkbank, Vakifbank
stories.summary: Turkey reshuffled the top ranks of two of its state-owned banks, installing new leadership at Turkiye Halk Bankasi and transferring its outgoing chief executive to Turkiye Vakiflar Bankasi, according to exchange filings late Thursday.

stories.headline: Bonds Fall, Stocks Steady Ahead of US-Iran Weekend Talks
Indonesia’s ‘Gasoline Godfather’ a Suspect in Second Graft Case
stories.summary: Indonesia has named reclusive commodities merchant Mohammad Riza Chalid as a suspect in a second corruption case tied to crude oil and fuel procurement, putting renewed scrutiny on legacy practices in one of Southeast Asia’s largest energy markets.

stories.headline: Who is Péter Magyar, the man leading the polls as Hungary prepares for election?

Japan retail investors make 25% of stock trades, highest in 12 years

'NATO not obliged to reopen the Strait,' Turkey's trade minister tells Euronews
stories.summary: US President Trump has issued an ultimatum to NATO countries to find a solution to reopening the Strait of Hormuz within days. Turkey – which has the second-largest army in NATO – says the alliance is not obliged to intervene.

stories.headline: From EV stations to heat pump grants: Inside France’s €240m plan to reduce its fossil fuel reliance
stories.summary: French Prime Minister Sébastian Lecornu says electrification is now a concern of ‘national interest’ and no longer just about climate change.

stories.headline: Vladimir Putin announces Orthodox Easter ceasefire with Ukraine
stories.summary: Kremlin proposes 32-hour ceasefire starting on Saturday afternoon – with Ukraine expected to agree to planVladimir Putin has declared a 32-hour ceasefire in Ukraine over the Orthodox Easter weekend, after an earlier call from Volodymyr Zelenskyy for a pause.The president’s decree, released by the Kremlin on Thursday, orders Russian forces to observe a ceasefire starting on 4pm Saturday and lasting until the end of Sunday. Continue reading...

stories.headline: Russia's president announces Orthodox Easter ceasefire with Ukraine, Kremlin says
stories.summary: Ukraine’s President Volodymyr Zelenskyy had previously signalled Kyiv's openness to a potential Easter truce.

stories.headline: Petrol and diesel prices rise again as concerns grow over ceasefire
stories.summary: Motoring groups have warned drivers not to expect a significant drop in costs soon.

stories.headline: Al Jazeera condemns killing of journalist in Israeli strike in Gaza
stories.summary: The Israeli military claims Mohammed Wishah was a "Hamas terrorist" - which the Qatar-based network has previously denied.

stories.headline: Ceasefire or no ceasefire, the Middle East's reshuffling is not yet done
stories.summary: Both sides have reason to end the war but share no common ground.

stories.headline: Trump criticises Nato as alliance chief describes meeting as 'very frank'
stories.summary: The US president says Nato "wasn't there when we needed them" during the Iran war.

stories.headline: He's Australia's most decorated soldier. Now he's at the centre of a historic war crimes case
stories.summary: Ben Roberts-Smith's case is not only unprecedented for Australia but "extraordinary" for the globe too, scholars say.

stories.headline: Ships remain cautious approaching Strait of Hormuz amid fragile ceasefire
stories.summary: Only a few vessels have crossed the strait since the US-Iran ceasefire deal, according to BBC Verify analysis.

stories.headline: Has US achieved its war objectives in Iran?
stories.summary: Key US objectives at the start of the war were to stop Iran getting a nuclear weapon and degrading its arsenal.

stories.headline: Melania Trump Says She Was Not Associated With Jeffrey Epstein
stories.summary: Responding to what she said were smears, the first lady said she never had knowledge of Jeffrey Epstein’s abuse and was not a victim of his. She called for a congressional hearing for his victims.

stories.headline: US First Lady Melania Trump denies connection to Epstein in rare speech
stories.summary: Melania calls for Congress to holding survivors' hearing but does not say why she chose to speak on the issue.

stories.headline: US First Lady Melania Trump says 'I was not a participant' in Epstein sexual abuse
stories.summary: Epstein died in federal custody in 2019 while awaiting trial on sex trafficking charges involving minors, but the scandal has repeatedly overshadowed Trump's second presidency.

stories.headline: Venezuela police use tear gas against protesters demanding better pay and pensions
stories.summary: The monthly minimum wage in Venezuela is 130 bolivars (€0.23), unchanged since 2022, and roughly 330 times lower than the UN poverty line of $3 (€2.56) a day.

stories.headline: Hot in the city: Energy crisis tests Singapore's air-con addiction
stories.summary: The rise in energy prices has hit Asia particularly hard as many nations are heavily reliant on Gulf oil.

stories.headline: Police in Venezuela block protesters calling for higher wages, pensions
stories.summary: Cost of living issues have persisted in Venezuela since the US abducted former President Nicolas Maduro in January.

stories.headline: CAF President Motsepe rejects Senegal’s corruption claim amid AFCON fallout
stories.summary: Senegal's government alleged corruption at African football's governing body after AFCON title awarded to Morocco.

stories.headline: French far-right leader romantically linked to Italian princess
stories.summary: Presidential hopeful Jordan Bardella was pictured on a Corsican beach with Princess Maria Carolina of Bourbon-Two Sicilies.

stories.headline: Argentina passes bill loosening protection of its glaciers
stories.summary: The new bill will make it easier for provincial governments to allow mining projects in glacier regions.

stories.headline: Italian government moves to oust Leonardo chief executive
stories.summary: Rome proposes that Roberto Cingolani be succeeded by company’s former chief commercial officer

stories.headline: Stocks Pause in Run-Up to Weekend US-Iran Talks: Markets Wrap
stories.summary: Stocks traded mixed at the end of a turbulent week as investors looked to talks between the US and Iran for signs a fragile truce can hold. Oil was set for its steepest weekly loss in nine months.

stories.headline: NLB Challenges Raiffeisen in Battle for Balkans Bank Addiko
stories.summary: Nova Ljubljanska banka d.d. said it plans to announce a purchase offer for shares in Balkans consumer lending specialist Addiko Bank AG, countering a similar proposal by Raiffeisen Bank International AG.

stories.headline: Ukrainian Railways Bond Restructuring Rejected by Investors
stories.summary: Initial debt-restructuring negotiations between Ukrainian Railways and its bondholders have ended without a deal after investors rejected an opening proposal from the state-owned rail operator, according to statements from both parties.

stories.headline: Oil Holds Gain After Attacks Lower Saudi Production Capacity
stories.summary: Oil rose a second day after Saudi Arabia said its production capacity has been reduced due to attacks on energy infrastructure, but futures remain on track for their biggest weekly loss since June.

stories.headline: Student Hit by Projectile During ‘No Kings’ Protest Lost an Eye, Lawyer Says
stories.summary: The student, Tucker Collins, 18, was observing demonstrators in Los Angeles when he was struck, the lawyer said.

stories.headline: Questions About N.C. Stabbing Suspect’s Mental Illness Loom Over Case
stories.summary: Decarlos Brown Jr., who is charged with murdering a Ukrainian immigrant, was found incapable of proceeding to state trial. A federal case against him will come first.

stories.headline: A GOP-aligned group is using Klan imagery to target Black voters
stories.summary: Civil rights rhetoric and images of Barack Obama are also being employed to persuade Virginians during the nationwide battle for U.S. House control.

stories.headline: Lidl to open 50 UK stores in year ahead – and its first pub
stories.summary: Almost 2,000 jobs will be created, with retailer vying to overtake Morrisons as Britain’s fifth largest supermarket Lidl is to open 50 new UK stores in the year ahead – as well as its first pub – as it aims to overtake Morrisons as the country’s fifth largest supermarket chain.The German-owned retailer has begun building a pub in east Belfast in response to strict local licensing laws that cap the number of premises that can sell alcohol. stories.headline: Ecuador hikes tariffs to 100-percent in feud with neighbour Colombia
stories.summary: Daniel Noboa has accused Colombia's Gustavo Petro of failing to take 'effective measures' against drug trafficking.

stories.headline: Iran ceasefire deal gives Trump a way out of war - but at a high cost
stories.summary: The path to the two-week ceasefire with Iran may have fundamentally altered the way the rest of the world views the US.

stories.headline: At least 182 killed across Lebanon in large wave of Israeli strikes
stories.summary: Attacks hit the southern suburbs of Beirut, southern Lebanon and the eastern Bekaa Valley, hours after a US-Iran ceasefire was announced.

stories.headline: How Pakistan helped secure a fragile ceasefire between the US and Iran
stories.summary: Pakistan has a historic relationship with Iran, but an agreement was far from certain, writes the BBC’s Caroline Davies

stories.headline: Kristi Noem’s luxury DHS jet to be used by other administration officials
stories.summary: The former homeland security secretary said the $70 million plane, which included a bedroom, was intended to carry out the president’s mass deportation agenda.

stories.headline: JD Vance backs Orbán's re-election bid in Budapest visit and hits out at EU
stories.summary: US Vice-President JD Vance intervenes in the campaign to give Orbán a ringing endorsement in Budapest.

stories.headline: Vietnam's leader To Lam strengthens power in unanimous assembly vote
stories.summary: Vietnam's communist party chief is now also the country's president - an unusual concentration of power.

stories.headline: The US refinery now processing Venezuelan oil
stories.summary: Chevron is now importing 250,000 barrels of crude per day from Venezuela.

stories.headline: Carnival fever hits Lagos as locals celebrate Afro-Brazilian heritage

stories.summary: The AI agent sparked a frenzy of "raising lobsters" in March, with users training the tool to suit their needs.
